indices.name: Pawn Index
indices.read: Pawn lenders vs the market — households monetizing possessions. Gold moved +2.5% (the control: a bullion rally can flatter these names).

indices.name: Dollar-Store Trade-Down
indices.read: Discounters vs mid-market retail. Outperformance = shoppers trading down, the squeeze climbing the income ladder.

indices.name: Debt-Collector Barometer
indices.read: The rare stocks that rally when loans go bad. Strength = a default wave being priced 2–3 quarters early.

indices.name: Dr. Copper
indices.read: The metal with a PhD in economics, divided by fear. Falling ratio = growth expectations deteriorating.

indices.name: Cardboard Box Index
indices.read: Everything ships in a box first. Box-maker weakness = a goods slowdown forming before GDP admits it. (Stock proxy; Fibre Box shipments are the deeper cut.)

indices.name: RV Canary
indices.read: RV makers — the most cancellable purchase there is. One Indiana county builds 80% of them; it led 2008 and 2020 by 6–12 months.

indices.name: Quiet Fear
indices.read: Crash insurance being bought while the surface stays calm. High SKEW + low VIX = fear without stress, smart money hedging quietly.

indices.name: Big Mac Index
indices.read: A burger as a currency-stress detector. The widest undervaluation flags the most-pressured currency. Most undervalued: Taiwan.

indices.name: Baltic Dry
indices.read: The price to hire a cargo ship — impossible to fake, every quote is a real cargo. It collapsed 94% before the 2008 trade crash.

indices.name: Tanker Tell
indices.read: US military tankers airborne right now vs a ~3-plane baseline. Refuelers can't hide — an anomalous surge preceded the June 2025 strikes by days. (316 mil aircraft tracked)
